profile.name: Tal Shterzer
profile.summary: Highly technical Engineering Manager and Systems Architect with 17+ years of progressive leadership, specializing in complex cloud migrations, enterprise modernization, and AI-driven product initiatives. Expert in translating business objectives into scalable technical roadmaps, establishing high-performance Agile cultures, and integrating AI Agents and LLM-powered automation to accelerate developer productivity and product quality. Deep expertise across Microsoft Azure, cloud-native architecture, and full-stack engineering. Drives technical excellence from strategic definition to code-level execution.

experience.title: Development Manager
highlights: Led, mentored, and coached an 11-engineer squad; own hiring, performance reviews, and career-development roadmaps; introduced Agile rituals, pair-programming, and structured mentoring for junior hires
Modernized and re-platformed a legacy on-prem monolith into a distributed cloud architecture on Microsoft Azure using .NET Core C# and React
Engineered a fully containerized ecosystem using Docker for independent, scalable deployment via Azure Container Apps
Orchestrated CI/CD pipelines from GitHub repositories to Microsoft Azure using GitHub Actions

Helmed a comprehensive BI and big-data solution, leading a cross-functional team of 4 Data Engineers and Scientists; migrated OLTP databases on Azure SQL to Snowflake OLAP via OneLake (Microsoft Fabric) and Apache Iceberg

Championed adoption of AI Agents across the development workflow, integrating LLM-powered coding agents (GitHub Copilot, Claude Code) to automate code reviews, test generation, and documentation; reduced manual overhead and improved sprint velocity

experience.title: Systems Architect & Senior Developer
highlights: Led a document-retrieval platform for company employees to search and retrieve customer account documents, including a Web API for integrating new document types and specialized source applications, on .NET 8.0
Designed, developed, and deployed software through Azure DevOps and GitHub
Led a Business-to-Business (B2B) project enabling download and upload of reports between businesses, built on .NET 8.0 and Microsoft Azure
Modernized systems by transitioning to Microservices with gRPC, Protocol Buffers, and .NET 8.0, resulting in significant improvement in system efficiency and reliability

experience.title: Server-Side Team Lead
highlights: Managed a Data Loss Prevention (DLP) security product, including Business Intelligence solutions for large organizations on Microsoft SSAS
Led a 4-member development team using Agile-Scrum methodology
Developed and defined the system in .NET 4.0, utilizing WPF, WCF Services, and SQL Server 2008, to streamline security processes and improve customer service

experience.title: Development Manager
highlights: Led a development project for the Ministry of Housing, managing a team of 8 (4 developers, 2 system analysts, 2 QA personnel), aiming to enhance the organizational information system
Designed and implemented solutions with .NET 3.5, BizTalk, and SQL Server, contributing to greater organizational efficiency

experience.title: Team Leader
highlights: Managed a development team of 9, leading development of a desktop application and website projects with emphasis on integrations with social networks including Facebook
Led development of various services using WPF, ASP.NET MVC, and JavaScript, leading to increased user satisfaction and expanded application usage

militaryService.role: Communications in an Armored Infantry Unit
